name: Frontend Engineering
description: UI/UX, design systems, accessibility, and component architecture
axioms: User-centered design: start with user needs, not technical capabilities
Accessibility is not optional: WCAG 2.1 AA is the minimum standard
Component-based architecture: UI is a tree of reusable, composable components
Responsive design: content adapts to viewport size and input method
Performance is a feature: 100ms response time feels instant, 1s keeps flow
Progressive enhancement: core functionality works without JavaScript
reasoning_patterns: Component decomposition: break UI into atomic/molecular/organism hierarchy
State management: local vs global, unidirectional data flow
Performance budget: set targets for LCP, FID, CLS (Core Web Vitals)
Accessibility audit: screen reader testing, keyboard navigation, color contrast
Design token system: colors, spacing, typography as variables
boundary_conditions: Does not cover backend APIs in depth (see 920)
Does not cover mobile native development (iOS/Android) in detail
3D graphics and game UI have specialized requirements
key_references: Norman, D. The Design of Everyday Things
Material Design / Human Interface Guidelines (design systems)
canonical_truths: JavaScript bundle size directly impacts time-to-interactive
CSS specificity wars are solved by design systems and utility classes
Single-page applications trade initial load time for navigation speed
Server-side rendering (SSR) improves first contentful paint and SEO
The best UI is invisible — users accomplish tasks without thinking about the interface
